cards.front: Who was the ancient Greek philosopher who first proposed that matter is made of indivisible particles called 'atomos'?
Who proposed the first modern atomic theory in 1808, describing atoms as solid, indivisible spheres?
Who discovered the electron using cathode ray tubes and proposed the 'plum pudding' model?
Who discovered the nucleus using the gold foil experiment and proposed that atoms are mostly empty space?
Who proposed the planetary model where electrons orbit the nucleus in specific energy levels?
What experiment proved the existence of a small, dense, positively charged nucleus?
What was the name of Thomson's atomic model?
What is the positively charged subatomic particle located in the nucleus?
What is the neutral subatomic particle located in the nucleus?
What is the negatively charged subatomic particle located outside the nucleus?
Where are protons and neutrons found in an atom?
Where are electrons found in an atom?
Which subatomic particle determines the identity of an element?
Which subatomic particle is involved in chemical bonding?
What is the number of protons in the nucleus of an atom called?
What is the total number of protons and neutrons in an atom's nucleus called?
What is the formula to calculate the number of neutrons in an atom?
In a neutral atom, the number of electrons equals what?
What are atoms of the same element that have different numbers of neutrons called?
What is the standard way to write an isotope showing the element symbol, mass number, and atomic number?
Isotopes of an element have the same number of what but different numbers of what?
cards.back: Same number of protons; different number of neutrons

cards.front: Which isotope of hydrogen has one proton and no neutrons?
Which isotope of hydrogen has one proton and one neutron?
Which isotope of hydrogen has one proton and two neutrons?
What is the weighted average mass of all the isotopes of an element called?
What is the formula used to calculate average atomic mass from isotopic masses and abundances?
What unit is used to express the mass of individual atoms?
What type of diagram shows an atom with a central nucleus and electrons arranged in concentric circular paths?
What is the maximum number of electrons that can fit in the first energy level?
What is the maximum number of electrons that can fit in the second energy level?
What is the main limitation of the Bohr model regarding electron paths?
cards.back: It incorrectly assumes electrons travel in fixed, circular orbits

cards.front: What is the main limitation of the Bohr model regarding multi-electron atoms?
cards.back: It works well for hydrogen but fails for atoms with more than one electron

cards.front: What is the unique pattern of lines produced when light from an element is passed through a prism?
What phenomenon occurs when electrons absorb energy, jump to higher levels, and release energy as light?
What provides evidence for the existence of fixed energy levels in atoms?
What are the fixed distances from the nucleus where electrons can exist called?
What are the subdivisions within energy levels where electrons are found?
What is the shape of an s sublevel?
What is the shape of a p sublevel?
What set of numbers is used to completely describe the location and energy of an electron in an atom?
